seed_problem: Given start+goal block configs, output a plan (move sequence) of minimal length respecting stacking rules; baseline = naive unstack-all. AI planning.
mutation: composite: keep the blocksworld plan-synthesis core but couple THREE mechanisms into one effort objective that a greedy cannot nail: (a) the classic settle-vs-move structure (crates already in a correct bottom prefix should never move), (b) a gantry TRAVEL cost |R-a|+|a-q| that turns move ORDERING into a sequencing/routing problem, and (c) a per-move handling cost H that trades ride distance against move count. The naive teardown (unstack-all-then-rebuild) is the calibration baseline; optimal restacking-plus-routing is intractable, so the best strategy must balance settling, direct placement, and ride minimization.
title: Warehouse Gantry Restacking: Minimum-Effort Crate Rearrangement
strategies: trivial: naive teardown -- park every crate to its own staging slot, then rebuild each goal stack bottom-to-top (reproduces the grader baseline B, ratio ~0.1)
greedy: settle-aware teardown -- leave already-correct bottom prefixes in place and tear down only misplaced crates to fixed far staging slots, then rebuild (~2*U moves, travel-naive)

strong: settle-aware teardown PLUS direct placement (put a crate straight onto its final position when the target column is already built to the right height) PLUS travel-aware nearest-safe staging -- fewer moves and shorter rides
invalid: emit a move with an out-of-range destination position -> infeasible (0)
